skill.name: conductor-status
skill.description: Display project status, active tracks, and next actions
skill.summary: Summarizes Conductor project progress, active tracks, blockers, and next tasks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks and example status markers as command execution or weak cryptography, but no executable code or cryptographic logic was found in SKILL.md. The skill does direct the agent to read project-local Conductor files, so the remaining risk is limited filesystem access within the user's workspace.

low_findings.title: Project Filesystem Read Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to check and read Conductor project files such as product.md, tracks.md, plan.md, metadata.json, and spec.md. This is expected for a project status skill, but it means the skill operates on local workspace data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-read behavior is explicitly described in the instructions. The paths are project-local Conductor files, with no network transfer or secret collection requested.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Cryptography Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code and fenced examples, not executable code. The weak cryptography alerts correspond to status-marker examples and template text, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains documentation and output templates only. I found no executable script, shell invocation, Ruby code, hashing function, or cryptographic operation.

content.value_statement: Project teams can lose track of active work, blockers, and next tasks across Conductor tracks. This skill summarizes progress, current focus, and next actions from Conductor project files.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether Conductor project files are present before reporting status.
Reads product, tracks, plan, metadata, and specification files under the Conductor directory.
Counts completed, in-progress, and pending tracks and tasks from checklist markers.
Identifies the active track, current phase, next pending task, and possible blockers.
Provides full project, single-track, quick, and JSON-style status formats.
limitations: Requires an initialized Conductor directory with expected file names.
Depends on consistent checklist markers in plan files.
Does not execute Conductor commands or modify project files.
Does not validate task completion against source code changes.

use_cases.title: Daily Project Check-In
use_cases.description: Summarize current progress, active work, and next tasks before a planning meeting.

use_cases.title: Track-Level Review
use_cases.description: Inspect one feature, bug, chore, or refactor track without reading every Conductor file manually.

use_cases.title: Blocker Triage
use_cases.description: Find blocked tasks, incomplete dependencies, and pending verification work across active tracks.

prompt_templates.title: Show Project Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-status to show the current project status and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Review One Track
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-status for the track auth_20250110 and summarize progress, blockers, and the next task.

prompt_templates.title: Find Active Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-status to identify the active track, current phase, current task, and the next two actions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Status Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor-status with detailed analysis and prepare a concise status report for a delivery review.

output_examples.input: Show the current Conductor project status.
output_examples.output: A project summary with total tracks, completed tasks, current focus, next actions, and blocker status.
A track summary with type, status, acceptance criteria, phase progress, related history, and next steps.

output_examples.input: Give me a quick status update.
output_examples.output: A short progress line with completed tasks, total tasks, active track, and current task.

best_practices: Keep Conductor checklist markers consistent across all plan files.
Run status checks before changing active tasks or creating new tracks.
Review blockers and dependencies before assigning next actions.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill before Conductor setup is complete.
Do not expect it to infer progress from source code without Conductor files.
Do not rely on it when track metadata or task markers are stale.

faq.question: What does this skill read?
faq.answer: It reads Conductor project files such as product.md, tracks.md, plan.md, metadata.json, and spec.md.

faq.question: Does it change files?
faq.answer: No. The instructions describe status reporting and do not request file modification.

faq.question: Can it show one track only?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide a track ID to request a single-track status view.

faq.question: Can it find blockers?
faq.answer: Yes. It looks for blocked task markers, incomplete dependencies, and failed verification tasks.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No executable shell command is defined. The backticks in SKILL.md are Markdown formatting and examples.

faq.question: What tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
